activities_projects.title: Case Study on South African Constitution
activities_projects.objective: Analyze how South Africa built a democratic constitution
steps: Study the transition from apartheid to democracy
Explain how negotiations helped create the constitution
Write a detailed case study
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students analyze democratic transitions and constitutional development
activities_projects.evaluation: Depth of analysis and clarity (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Debate on Majority Rule and Minority Rights
activities_projects.objective: Understand balance between majority rule and minority protection
steps: Divide students into two groups
One group argues for majority rule
The other explains protection of minority rights
Conduct a debate
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand democratic balance between majority power and minority rights

activities_projects.title: Research on Constituent Assembly Debates
activities_projects.objective: Study the discussion process during constitution making
steps: Find information about Constituent Assembly debates
Identify major issues discussed
Write a report summarizing these debates
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand how democratic discussion shapes laws

activities_projects.title: Comparative Study of Constitutions
steps: Study the constitutions of India, USA and South Africa
Compare their Preambles
Identify similarities and differences
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students develop global understanding of constitutional systems
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy and depth of comparison (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Analyze the Preamble
activities_projects.objective: Interpret the philosophical meaning of the Preamble
steps: Write the Preamble in your notebook
Explain each term such as Sovereign, Socialist and Secular
Discuss how these values affect governance

activities_projects.title: Role Play of Constituent Assembly
steps: Assign roles of leaders like Ambedkar and Nehru
Discuss issues such as equality and rights
Conduct a simulated Constituent Assembly session

activities_projects.title: Research on Constitutional Amendments
activities_projects.objective: Understand why constitutions change over time
steps: Find examples of constitutional amendments
Explain why these amendments were needed
Write a report
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand flexibility in constitutional systems

activities_projects.title: Essay on Democratic Values
activities_projects.objective: Analyze values like justice and equality
steps: Write an essay explaining justice, liberty and equality
Give examples from daily life
Present the essay in class
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students connect constitutional values to real life

activities_projects.title: Critical Discussion on Social Equality
activities_projects.objective: Understand the challenges of equality in society
steps: Discuss social and economic inequalities
Explain how the constitution tries to reduce them
Write conclusions
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students analyze social justice goals in the Constitution

activities_projects.title: Design a Model Constitution
steps: Create a simple constitution for an imaginary country
Include rights, powers of government and values
Present the model constitution
